name: JackTheFlipper — RaspyJack + Flipper bridge (Pi Zero 2 W)
description: Pi OS Lite (Bookworm, 64-bit) with the full RaspyJack toolkit (LCD-driven, 231 payloads) AND the Sinsera Flipper Companion bridge baked in. Boot, wait, both work — standalone via the 1.44" LCD HAT, or remotely via Ark → SSH Runner.

preconfigured.user: peta (no password — SSH-key only, sudo NOPASSWD)
preconfigured.raspyjack: /opt/raspyjack/ (extracted by jacktheflipper-install.service on first network-up boot)

first_boot_sequence: Phase 1 (~30s): kernel + firstrun.sh — creates peta user, SSH key, hostname, WiFi, enables sshd. Reboots.
Phase 2 (~8-12 min): jacktheflipper-install.service runs once network is up — apt update + install_raspyjack.sh + Flipper bridge + udev. Reboots once more for SPI dtoverlay.
Phase 3 (~20s): final boot — RaspyJack ready on LCD HAT, Flipper bridge ready over USB. SSH to peta@jacktheflipper.local.
